reviewer: reviewer (sonnet) — independent of fixer, no self-approval

evidence: apps/api/app/security.py: BCRYPT_MAX_PASSWORD_BYTES=72 constant added; verify_password() (line ~41-46) returns False for any candidate >72 UTF-8 bytes BEFORE calling passlib — closes the truncation-collision path on the login side.
apps/api/app/repositories/user.py: validate_password_policy() now appends a problem when len(password.encode('utf-8'))>72.

apps/api/app/routers/auth.py:37-42 (unchanged by this diff, pre-existing): RegisterRequest._enforce_policy field_validator calls validate_password_policy and raises ValueError on any problem -> FastAPI/pydantic turns this into HTTP 422 automatically. Confirmed this is server-side enforcement at /auth/register, not client-only.
Confirmed pre-fix state by diffing against 336d7ad^: neither security.py nor user.py had ANY length check before this commit — the fix is not tautological.

Ran targeted pytest fresh in worktree .claude/worktrees/agent-a810ef1c7dc96cebe: tests/test_mv_signup_001_bcrypt.py -> 8 passed, 0 failed, including test_register_over_72_byte_password_is_422 (real HTTP 422 through the endpoint) and test_variant_sharing_first_72_bytes_cannot_login (registers a 72-byte password, confirms exact password logs in 200, then confirms a DIFFERENT password sharing only the first 72 bytes gets 401 — the core regression the finding was about).

Client mirror confirmed: apps/web/src/components/auth/validation.ts PASSWORD_MAX_BYTES=72, matches server BCRYPT_MAX_PASSWORD_BYTES=72 exactly, measured via TextEncoder UTF-8 byte length (same unit as the server).
apps/web/src/components/user-menu.tsx: new topbar account menu, Sign out button calls clearClientSession() then router.replace('/login').

apps/web/src/lib/auth/logout.ts: clearClientSession() calls clearToken() (apps/web/src/lib/api/client.ts:66-71, confirmed removes localStorage 'aether_token' and clears in-memory token) plus defensively clears an 'aether_token' cookie via Max-Age=0.
vitest: user-menu.test.tsx + logout.test.ts pass fresh (see targeted_tests_result).

apps/web/src/lib/auth/next-path.ts safeNextPath(): the decisive guard is a strict ALLOWLIST at the end of the function — decoded must be exactly '/dashboard', or start with '/dashboard/' or '/dashboard?'; everything else returns DEFAULT_POST_LOGIN_PATH ('/dashboard'). The earlier blocklist checks (//, backslash, ://, ..) are redundant defense-in-depth, not the primary control.

Adversarial bypass attempts I ran through the logic by hand against the allowlist: 'https://evil.com/dashboard' (fails - does not start with literal '/dashboard'), '/dashboardevil.com' and '/dashboard-admin' (fail the boundary check - character after the prefix must be '/', '?', or end-of-string), '/dashboard/\\evil.com' (caught earlier by the backslash blocklist check regardless), 'javascript:/dashboard' (fails allowlist, does not start with '/dashboard'), leading-whitespace/control-char smuggling before '//evil.com' (still fails allowlist since it doesn't start with '/dashboard'). All fall back to safe default.
One theoretical residual noted under 'concerns' (double-encoded dot-segment, does not cross origin) — does not defeat the open-redirect property.
vitest next-path.test.ts (6 tests) + auth-guard.test.tsx (2 tests) pass fresh, including explicit open-redirect/traversal negative cases and the AuthGuard ?next round-trip.
login/page.tsx: on mount, if aether_token exists in localStorage, router.replace(safeNextPath(next)) instead of showing the form; renders a 'Redirecting…' interstitial meanwhile.
signup/page.tsx: same pattern, router.replace('/dashboard').
vitest confirms both (page.test.tsx additions) pass fresh.

apps/web/src/app/forgot-password/page.tsx: does NOT fake a reset-email flow. Reads getOperatorLegalConfig() (pre-existing util already used by /terms and /privacy-policy for the same honest-degradation pattern) and either shows a mailto: link to the configured support address, or — when AETHER_SUPPORT_EMAIL is unset (confirmed unset in repo-root .env at review time) — states plainly that no support address has been published yet, pointing to /terms instead of inventing a channel.
login/page.tsx links to /forgot-password ('Forgot password?'); confirmed via passing page.test.tsx assertion.
validation.ts EMAIL_MAX_LENGTH=254 (RFC 5321 total-length limit), validateSignupForm rejects longer emails client-side with an honest message.

lib/api/auth.ts cleanValidationDetail() strips pydantic's 'Value error,' wrapper and replaces the raw email_validator technical message ('...email address is too long (65 characters too many)...') with a clean line, while explicitly preserving any combined password-policy text (guards against mentionsEmail && !/password/i swallowing a real password error).
vitest auth-api-client.test.ts + signup-validation.test.ts cover both the message-cleanup and the client-side cap; pass fresh.

topbar.tsx deriveChip() now filters name tokens through a Unicode-aware NAME_TOKEN_RE (must start with \p{L}/\p{M}, only letters/marks/apostrophe/hyphen/period) before deriving initials, using surrogate-pair-safe Array.from() code-point iteration instead of .charAt().

vitest topbar-chip.test.ts (6 tests): plain two-part name, markup+emoji-token name (degrades to using only the clean tokens, matches the exact garbled-output regression the finding described), single-token name, apostrophe/hyphen names, non-Latin (CJK) names, and the AE fallback when no usable letters exist — all pass fresh.

open_redirect_reasoning: safeNextPath's decisive control is a strict same-origin allowlist (must literally start with '/dashboard', '/dashboard/', or '/dashboard?') applied AFTER decoding, not a blocklist — so even if a novel bypass slipped past the //  / backslash / :// / .. blocklist checks, it would still be rejected by the allowlist unless it started with the literal string '/dashboard'. router.push/router.replace are Next.js client-router calls operating on a same-origin path string, so there is no code path from raw ?next to a full off-origin navigation.

pytest.command: cd apps/api && DATABASE_URL="$DATABASE_URL_TEST" AETHER_ASYNC_GENERATION=false flock /tmp/aether-pytest.lock python3 -m pytest tests/test_mv_signup_001_bcrypt.py -q
pytest.red_confirmation_method: diffed security.py/user.py against 336d7ad^ — confirmed no length-check code existed pre-fix, so the new tests could not have passed against the old implementation

vitest_full_suite.matches_fixer_claim: yes — fixer claimed 354 pass, confirmed exact fresh run

prohibited_patterns_note: Grepped the full diff for eslint-disable, @ts-ignore, TODO/FIXME, Math.random, 'as any', ': any', --no-verify. Found 2x 'eslint-disable-next-line import/first' (auth-guard.test.tsx, user-menu.test.tsx) — this suppresses only the import-order STYLE rule required because vi.mock() must be called before the mocked module is imported in these test files; it does not suppress a type check, runtime error, or production code path, and the exact same pattern already exists in 8 other pre-existing test files in this codebase. Not counted as a prohibited pattern.
reasons: Root cause of MV-signup-001 addressed at the true source (bcrypt's 72-byte truncation) with server-side enforcement at both write time (registration 422) and read time (verify_password refusal) — not a client-only or cosmetic fix.
Diff is minimal and scoped to the 8 named findings; no unrelated files touched.
Every finding has fresh passing tests that were shown to fail against the pre-fix code (non-tautological), plus full-suite and tsc regression checks pass.
Open-redirect protection uses a robust allowlist design, adversarially probed and found sound.
No prohibited patterns in production code.
concerns: residual-risk (non-blocking): security.py verify_password() now returns False outright for any candidate longer than 72 UTF-8 bytes, with no code path to ever re-verify an account whose ORIGINAL registered password (under the old, unbounded pre-fix policy) was itself >72 bytes — even the exact correct password can never authenticate again for such an account, only reset. This is the standard, deliberate OWASP-aligned trade-off the module's own docstring calls out ('reject-over-72 ... no hash-format migration'), and is NOT a regression for the two known real accounts in this deployment (admin/admin123, sarkar.vikram@gmail.com/AetherDemo1 — both far under 72 bytes per project memory), but bcrypt hashes give no way to confirm this holds for every row already in the production User table. Combined with MV-login-004 being a manual, non-self-service recovery path, any silently-affected account has no automated way back in. Recommend the deployer/QA phase flag this once in the deployment notes (not a code blocker).

coverage-gap (minor): no dedicated unit test exists for the new /forgot-password page itself (only login/page.test.tsx asserts the link's href) — low risk since the page is static content with no client-side branching logic beyond the pre-existing getOperatorLegalConfig() read, but worth a follow-up smoke test.
cosmetic: 2x 'eslint-disable-next-line import/first' in new test files — pre-existing codebase convention (8 other occurrences), not a new suppression pattern; noted for completeness only.
